Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to raise a concern regarding the customer service my daughter and I experienced during our visit to your store on 15 April.

We were shopping for clothing and shoes for my grandchildren. During our time in the store, we noticed that two staff members serving the public appeared to ignore us, which made us feel unwelcome.

When my granddaughter selected a small toy to purchase, my daughter proceeded to the counter to pay using a gift card. The staff member advised, in a dismissive tone, that the EFTPOS machine might not accept gift cards and suggested using cash or a debit card instead. My daughter politely asked if she could try the gift card first.

Although the payment was successfully processed, the staff member spoke in a rude and demanding manner, asking her to “do it again,” which was unnecessary and uncomfortable. It felt as though the staff member was frustrated that the gift card worked.

We found this interaction disappointing. There was no reason for the tone or behaviour displayed, and it did not reflect the level of customer service we would expect from your brand.

Customer service plays an important role in the overall shopping experience, and I hope this feedback can be used to improve how customers are treated in the future.

To be honest, it feels less painful standing barefoot on rocks than wearing these shoes. That’s how uncomfortable they are.

I’m extremely disappointed, especially considering the hype around this model. I feel like I’ve wasted my hard-earned money on something that doesn’t deliver what it promises.

I would not recommend these shoes to anyone based on my experience.

Walked into the store this morning to buy a birthday gift, looked through the product I wanted, and yet no one wanted to take my money.

The girl you had zoned at the front of the store (on the left-hand side when entering) was in activewear with long straight hair. She was leaning on a tall table, tapping her hand loudly on the surface, with her back deliberately turned to the entrance and anyone walking in. When she was customer-facing, her eyes were down, intentionally ignoring both myself and other customers.

I saw three other staff members perfectly positioned on the floor, yet not one person acknowledged me. One girl at the back left near the fitting rooms stared out into the store. We made eye contact and instead of a small smile or acknowledgment, she looked down and ignored me. Another team member behind the counter saw me browsing the sale Jordan jerseys, clearly looking to buy a gift, and also ignored me before turning to chat with someone in the shoe area.

I lapped a section of the store ready to make a purchase, but instead watched myself and other customers receive absolutely nothing.

If your team is missing budgets, KPIs, or conversion targets, this is why.

I walked across to Stateside Sport where a team member, who had his back to me while hanging a shirt, immediately noticed me and greeted me within 30 seconds. He was working alone, and he cared.

People don’t have a lot of money to spend right now. When they choose to walk into your store and spend their hard-earned dollars, it is the team’s responsibility to deliver at least the basics. Allowing customers to walk from the front to the back of a large store, browse, and leave without even a simple acknowledgment is a complete failure.

I am extremely disappointed with my experience at this store. I came in specifically to buy shoes for my child, and considering their prices are far from cheap, I expected at least basic customer service. Instead, I was met with total indifference.
I spent 10 minutes in the store after picking out a few models, waiting for someone to help with sizing. There were four staff members present: one was serving a customer, two were behind the counter, and another was busy with stock. Despite the store not being busy at all, no one approached me.
When I finally asked a free cashier for assistance, I was told I couldn't be helped unless I went back to the entrance to "check in" via a QR code and join a "virtual queue." Are you serious?
There were more staff members than customers in the shop, yet they insisted on a digital queue for a simple request. I wasn't going to waste my time on a "fictional" line when people were literally standing around.
I walked out and took my $300 to a competitor nearby, where I was served and finished my purchase in under two minutes.
If you value your time and expect decent service for the premium prices you pay, avoid this place. Their "system" is a joke and their staff's attitude is even worse. Never coming back.

In short: Decent selection, but staff need better product training.

Visited recently to buy two pairs of shoes for my young child. The store has a good range, but the customer service experience was disappointing.

When I asked one of the free staff members for a shoe in 15.5 cm — a completely normal way for parents to describe their child's foot measurement — he looked confused and told me he needed a UK or US size instead. While I understand that UK sizing is the standard in-store format in Australia, parents routinely measure their kids' feet in centimetres at home, and staff at a children's shoe store should be trained to handle that conversion without making the customer feel like they've asked something unusual. Instead, I had to walk outside the store to find mobile signal, Google the conversion myself, and show him my phone. He then retrieved the shoes, handed over, and walked away without a word.

For the second pair, another staff member let us know that a shoe had been accidentally placed in the wrong section and wasn't actually available in our size — frustrating, but at least that was communicated clearly.

The store could be a great place to shop for kids' shoes, but when basic fitting knowledge is lacking and the service feels indifferent, it's hard to recommend. Hopefully management can invest in proper staff training, especially around helping parents who come in with foot measurements rather than size numbers.

one_star_reviews.text:
We are extremely disappointed with the quality of workmanship and after-sales service provided.

1. We previously reported serious issues with excessive gaps around the doors, with some gaps measuring over 6mm, causing significant wind drafts and poor insulation throughout the house. When this was raised with the responsible coordinator , we were simply told that “the gap should be between 3–5mm, so if it is 6mm then it is only 1mm over” and that it was “not a safety issue or serious defect.”
We find this response extremely irresponsible and dismissive. No inspection was arranged, nobody came to properly assess the issue onsite, and our concerns were brushed aside simply because they personally decided it was not considered a “serious defect.” Regardless of whether they classify it as a major defect, obvious workmanship issues and customer concerns should still be properly investigated and addressed instead of ignored

2. After handover, the backyard was left full of construction waste and rubbish that should have been cleaned up by the builder before completion.

3. A damaged water pipe was reported multiple times, yet nobody came to repair it. Because we urgently needed to proceed with our concrete work, we were forced to replace the pipe ourselves at our own expense.

4. There are multiple damaged and defective areas on the slab/concrete work. We reported these issues over 3 months ago, but until today no proper repairs or action have been taken.

5. The installed window sizes are obviously undersized and do not fit properly, resulting in excessive noise and movement whenever there is strong wind. It also creates serious security concerns, as the windows appear much easier to force open or break into from outside. We raised this concern with the responsible supervisor, fbut the issue was ignored and no solution was provided.

6. As shown in the attached photo, there is a big gap between the garage floor and the wall on the right-hand side only. It is obvious that this area has been poorly patched or filled afterward, resulting in the garage door sitting too far away from the wall and leaving an excessively large gap around the garage door.

This issue was reported, however the responsible coordinator simply stated that there was “nothing they could do” and told us to purchase and install a brush seal ourselves to cover the gap. We find this completely unacceptable. A major construction and installation issue should not be pushed back onto the homeowner to fix at their own cost, especially when it appears to be caused by poor workmanship and incorrect alignment during construction.

Overall, the build quality, communication, and post-handover support have been extremely poor. We expected a professional standard but instead have been left dealing with ongoing defects, delays, and unresolved safety concerns.

Dismissive, unprofessional, and not worth the time or money. I strongly recommend going elsewhere.
I attended this clinic for a scheduled iron infusion. I had been instructed to obtain a prescription, have it filled, and bring the medication with me, which I did.
Multiple attempts were made by two nurses and then a doctor to insert the IV, all of which failed initially. When the doctor eventually accessed the vein, there were no bungs available to stop the blood flow, resulting in my blood pouring through the needle and all over me, the bed, the floor, and the doctor’s shoes. The doctor commented on his shoes while my blood was still actively flowing.
The needle was removed and staff began calling nearby practices to locate the required equipment. Prior to my arrival, another patient left after being unable to have their IV inserted. The nurses had already prepared that patient’s iron infusion and openly discussed (within earshot, separated only by a curtain) swapping their medication with mine and storing my prescribed iron in the fridge for later use.
Eventually the correct equipment was sourced and the infusion proceeded.
I would have left if my iron levels were not critically low, it was close to the weekend, and I had already waited a significant time for this appointment. The lack of basic equipment, poor infection control practices, and unprofessional conduct were extremely concerning.

In response to your dismissive response:
I can confirm that I did attend your clinic for an iron infusion and brought a prescribed medication as instructed.
The events described in my review occurred during that appointment, including multiple failed IV attempts, uncontrolled bleeding due to lack of appropriate equipment, and staff discussing the use of another patient’s prepared medication within earshot.
I find it concerning that instead of addressing these serious issues, your response focuses on denying my attendance.
I would welcome you to review your records more thoroughly, including appointment logs and staff present on the day. Patient safety, infection control, and privacy should be taken seriously in all circumstances.
